description: Directed service dependency graph. An edge {from: A, to: B} means A calls/produces-to B (request/data flow). http-receiver receives ingest and produces to kafka-metric-ingestor, which enqueues onto ed-olapdb-mt-1-metric-iq, consumed by metric-ingestor-1.

edges.note: produces to the queue; metric-ingestor-1 is the CONSUMER

note: Data flows A->B. CAUSALLY, when the downstream consumer metric-ingestor-1 is slow, the queue backlogs and backpressure flows BACKWARD: consumer -> producer (kafka-metric-ingestor) -> edge (http-receiver). The edge's load spike is the EFFECT, not the cause.
